exemplar.title:
To what extent does consuming âRed Bullâ energy drink effect
the heart rate (BPM) and systolic and diastolic blood pressure (kpa) of
16-17 years old elite athletes immediately after consumption?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent does consuming âRed Bullâ energy drink effect
the heart rate (BPM) and systolic and diastolic blood pressure (kpa) of
16-17 years old elite athletes immediately after consumption?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Genuine linkage of investigation to personal dance practice and caffeine use
Evidence of independent initiative in designing forms, recruiting participants, and implementing repeated trials
Clear articulation of personal significance and curiosity regarding Red Bull consumption
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands (evidence of engagement, personal significance, and initiative) clearly meet the highest band. The studentâs personal connection to dance and caffeine, their motivation to refine consumption habits, and tangible input in method design justify the full 2 marks.

strengths: Relevant and mostly focused research question supported by extensive literature review
Comprehensive background on caffeine pharmacokinetics and related studies
Some awareness of safety and ethical considerations via PAR-Q and parental consent
weaknesses: Imprecise phrasing of the research question and inconsistent units (kPa vs mmHg)
Methodology lacks placebo control, randomization, and thorough ethical safeguards (side-effect monitoring, data privacy)
Confounders table is truncated and procedural details (timer accuracy, randomization) are incomplete
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the research question and background are well-researched (bands 3â4), significant methodological and ethical limitations (lack of placebo, partial safety planning) constrain the overall band to the lower half. Thus, 3 marks appropriately reflect the mid-band performance.

strengths: Sufficient raw data from 14 participants supporting paired analysis
Correct use of standard deviation and paired t-test to address research hypotheses
Instrument uncertainties are acknowledged and interpretation aligns broadly with statistical outcomes
weaknesses: Inconsistent data processing conventions (Î£ notation errors, CoV interpretation)
Uncertainty propagation is brief and not integrated into final conclusions
Some overstatements in hypothesis acceptance and mislabeling of null/alternative hypotheses
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The data set is of good quality and quantity (band 3â4), and inferential methods are appropriate, though with some inaccuracies. Interpretation is broadly valid but incomplete. These factors warrant the upper half of band 3â4, so 4 marks are justified.

strengths: Relevant conclusion linked to data with acknowledgment of variability
Detailed discussion of confounders and methodological limitations
Multiple realistic and well-linked suggestions for improvement
weaknesses: Conclusion statements are somewhat contradictory and could be streamlined
Comparisons to scientific context are descriptive rather than critically integrated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation shows solid methodological awareness (band 3â4) and offers detailed improvements (band 5â6). However, the contradictory conclusion and only descriptive contextual comparison limit full marks. A score of 4 balances these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Overall clear presentation with appropriate headings, tables, and figures
Logical structure following IA conventions with all necessary sections
Consistent use of SEHS terminology and citation style
weaknesses: Occasional formatting artefacts, lengthy background sections, and narrative density
Mix-ups in units (kPa vs mmHg) and inconsistent notation (hypothesis subscripts)
Some irrelevant details (marketing expenditure) obscure focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report is well organized and uses proper terminology (band 3), but minor clarity and focus issues prevent full marks. The overall clarity, structure, relevance, and conventions merit 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Instrument uncertainties are listed (Â±1 bpm, Â±0.1 kPa) but they are not propagated or discussed in depth.  Brief acknowledgment meets âsome considerationâ rather than thorough treatment.

The student compares findings with several published studies on caffeine and placebo effects, demonstrating relevance, yet the discussion is descriptive rather than critically integrated, fitting âsome relevant comparisonâ.

Report follows standard IA structure (IntroductionâMethodâDataâAnalysisâEvaluation), includes tables and appendices, and all required information is present.  Some repetition and oversized background sections reduce tight organisation, yet overall structure is sound.

Multiple realistic improvements are proposed: adding a placebo control, synchronising test times, using fatigue scales, enlarging sample to non-athletes, and increasing equipment availability.  Suggestions are detailed and clearly linked to identified weaknesses, satisfying the 5-6 band (assigned 5 marks).

The question is relevant and mostly focused (immediate effect of 50 ml Red Bull on HR and BP in 16-17-year-old elite athletes).  However, wording is slightly awkward (units in kPa, âeffectâ not defined) and scope broadens to three dependent variables, so full precision is not yet achieved.

Confounding variables (sleep, prior activity, behaviour) and equipment misuse are analysed in detail, showing solid methodological awareness.  However, quantitative estimation of their impact is missing, so the highest band is not fully met.

Methodology is generally suitableâpre/post design, repeat measures, control list, PAR-Qâbut lacks a placebo, randomisation and adequate rest interval for caffeine absorption, so several significant factors remain unaddressed.  Hence it fits âmainly appropriate with limitationsâ.

Extensive literature is reviewed (caffeine pharmacokinetics, taurine, sugar, prior studies).  Although occasionally digressive, the information is largely relevant and supports the biological rationale for selecting HR/BP as markers, meeting the upper end of the âmainly appropriateâ band.

Most content is relevant to the RQ; however, marketing expenditure figures and extended ingredient descriptions add superfluous detail, slightly obscuring focus.  Relevance is adequate but not exemplary.

Interpretation matches statistics (HR p=0.05; DIA p=0.03) and recognises methodological weaknesses, yet claims about rejecting/accepting multiple hypotheses and linkage to marketing slogans are somewhat overstated.  Overall interpretation is broadly valid but incomplete.

Headings, tables and figures aid comprehension, but lengthy narrative, occasional formatting artefacts and some mathematical typos impede full clarity.  Presentation is generally clear with minor errors.

The studentâs interest in energy-drink use is woven through the report, linking personal dance practice and everyday reliance on caffeine to the choice of variables and sample.  References to personally preparing forms, recruiting classmates and repeating trials across three days show meaningful independent thinking beyond teacher-led guidance.

Processing includes means, SD, CoV and paired t-tests.  Calculations are shown, yet several values (e.g., CoV interpretation, SD notation) reveal inconsistency and occasional misunderstanding.  Thus processing is appropriate but not consistently accurate.

The introduction explicitly explains why the claim âgives you wingsâ matters to the student as a teenager and dancer who consumes Red Bull.  Curiosity is clear in the wish to âreduce my consumption or continue using itâ, demonstrating direct personal significance rather than a generic rationale.

Three full data sets from 14 participants (42 paired measurements) are presented in detailed tables.  Minor gaps and transcription errors exist but overall the quantity and relevance are adequate to support conclusions, aligning with the upper half of the 3-4 band.

Design elementsâchoosing 50 ml doses, arranging SSC test sessions, creating a digital PAR-Q, and manually deriving mean/SD/CoV/t-testsâindicate sustained personal input in design, implementation and presentation, not merely following a published protocol.

Safety and ethics are considered via PAR-Q, parental consent, non-invasive equipment and IB policy reference.  Environmental note on e-forms is included, but no discussion of caffeine side-effects monitoring or data privacy.  This shows âsome awarenessâ rather than comprehensive coverage.

Appropriate SEHS terminology (systolic, diastolic, PAR-Q) is used and citations follow a consistent style, yet units mix-ups (kPa vs mmHg) and occasional notation errors persist.  Conventions are mostly correct with minor issues.

A clear concluding statement is provided and linked to data, but support is weakened by high variability and acknowledged design flaws.  Evidence is present but not fully convincing, placing it in the middle band.

annotations.comment: The research questionâs wording is imprecise and conflates units. Redefine âeffectâ operationally and use consistent units (e.g., mmHg instead of kPa) for clarity and focus.
The title and section header clearly frame the investigation and link personal interests to the study of Red Bullâs physiological impact.
The extensive marketing expenditure detail distracts from the scientific background. Focus more tightly on caffeineâs physiological effects rather than corporate sponsorship figures.
Linking personal dance practice and ED consumption shows genuine engagement. The student effectively grounds the study in personal experience.
The background mixes kPa with BPM without clarifying conversion. Clearly explain why kPa is used for BP and note the common mmHg equivalence.
The research and background section is comprehensive, reviewing caffeine kinetics and prior studies to support the choice of HR and BP as meaningful markers.
The background paragraphs are lengthy and dense. Introduce subheadings or bullet points to improve readability and emphasize key concepts.
Restating the research question mid-report adds redundancy. Remove duplication or refine this section to focus on justification rather than repetition.
Hypotheses numbering is inconsistent (Hâ/Hâ/Hâ formatting differs). Standardize notation and ensure subscript consistency for clarity.
No placebo or randomization is included. Introduce a placebo control and counterbalance order to strengthen the methodology and reduce expectancy effects.
The confounding variables table is truncated and lacks complete descriptions. Expand this section to detail each confounderâs potential magnitude of effect.
PAR-Q procedure is described, but there is no plan for monitoring caffeine side effects or adverse reactions during testing. Include ongoing health checks.
Data privacy and consent are not discussed. Clarify how participant responses and data will be stored and anonymized to meet ethical standards.
The timerâs accuracy is noted as Â±0:00:01 ms, which is unrealistic for a mobile device. Confirm timing precision and report units correctly (s, not ms).
Procedural steps omit randomizing participant order. Randomization would reduce systematic bias and improve the validity of comparisons.
Only two participantsâ raw data are shown; the rest is in the appendix. Include a concise summary table of all 14 pre/post values to aid interpretation.
The mean equation uses Î£^X notation incorrectly. Use Î£ X_i in the numerator for proper mathematical convention.
The standard deviation formula is correctly presented, demonstrating solid understanding of variability measures.
The paired t-test equation is clearly shown, supporting appropriate choice of inferential method for paired data.
Clarify whether a two-tailed or one-tailed test was used and justify the choice based on hypothesesâ directionality.
Hypothesis testing claims mix up Hâ/Hâ labels and incorrectly describe rejecting and accepting the null. Align statements precisely with p-value outcomes.
The discussion of placebo effects and expectancy shows critical insight and enriches interpretation, linking findings to broader psychophysiological context.
The evaluation of methodological limitations is detailed and reflective, demonstrating awareness of confounders and instrumentation challenges.
The final conclusion contradicts itself by first denying HR and SYS BP effects then affirming frequent DIA BP changes. Streamline to present consistent findings.
Reference formatting is inconsistent, with incomplete URLs and missing DOIs. Adopt a uniform citation style and verify all links.
Table 1 in the appendix succinctly summarizes Red Bullâs ingredients and quantities, effectively supporting the background discussion.
